sections.paragraph: fem. fishing, fishery. סִירֹות דּוּגָה fish-hooks, harpoons. Amo 4:2, “ye shall be drawn with hooks, וְאַהֲרִיתְכֶן בְּסִירֹות דּוּגָה and your posterity with fishing-hooks,” an image drawn from taming beasts, into the noses of which hooks and rings were put. Comp. Isa 37:29, “I will put my hook into thy nose … and will turn thee back whence thou camest.” The reason why fishing-hooks should be mentioned is shewn by Eze 29:4 Job 40:26 comp. Oedmann, Verm. Sammll. aus d. Naturkunde, v. 5. The larger fishes, when taken, used to have rings put into their nostrils by which they were again let down into the water.
